prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial is shown in black and white and appears to take place mainly in a domestic kitchen/dining setting. A “Nostalgic X’er” watermark is visible in the lower-right corner of the sampled frames.

At the beginning, a woman stands at a kitchen counter preparing food, possibly mixing or handling ingredients in a bowl. Kitchen shelves, utensils, and a window are visible behind her. A man enters or stands in the background near a doorway, facing toward her.

The next sampled scene cuts to a close view inside or near a refrigerator or storage shelf. A person, seen from behind and to the side, reaches toward several glass Coca-Cola bottles standing upright on a shelf. The Coca-Cola logos are visible on the bottles.

Later, the setting shifts to a dining table. The woman and the man are seated together at a table with food and tableware spread out in front of them. The woman holds a Coca-Cola bottle, while the man pours a dark beverage from a bottle into a glass. They both appear to be smiling or pleasantly engaged with the meal.

The final frame returns to a close-up of multiple glass Coca-Cola bottles lined up on a shelf. A hand reaches in and grips one of the bottles, as if taking it from the row. The Coca-Cola branding is clearly visible on several bottles.
